fullText: google's new AI model just changed voice agents forever Hey so I think that these scissors are broken These two different pieces came apart Do you know how I might be able to fix this i see It looks like these are designed to come apart for cleaning If you align the rivet on one blade with the slot on the other they should slide back together securely Can you give that a try there we go I think I got it working Perfect That's exactly how they connect Hey Gemini how's it going just wanted to make sure you can see me and that we can have a conversation I can see you You're in a room with light gray walls a shelf behind you with some trophies and a microphone in front of you Everything looks good from my end ready to chat What's on your mind what kind of microphone do you think I have that looks like a Shore MV7 Probably connected to that road arm Is that right yeah that is You can see right here I've got the road And you can see right here this is the Shore M7 All right So Google just dropped Gemini 3.1 Flash Live which is their latest voice model that has a lot of upgrades In fact you can even see right here in this video they're calling it their biggest upgrade yet The whole idea is that this is way more human because it's no longer doing speech to text and then text to speech It's just straight up speech to speech and it can also see things as you guys saw in that quick demo beforehand Now this first benchmark is about multi-step function calling which it has outperformed the previous Gemini 2.5 Flash model by about 19% here as you can see So here's a quick example and I'm going to call out some of the benefits So we know that it's speech to speech We know that there is less latency and we know that it can work in noisy environments So there's the first one You might hear you know on the side of the road things like traffic things like horns I can look you up with your registration plate and postcode instead Could you provide those for me please whoa And it's still able to be unfased by stuff like that It also has a higher level of accuracy when it comes to alpha numeric strings And because it's interpreting speech rather than speech being transcribed into text there's way more contextual awareness because the audio is coming through and it can understand things like sarcasm or stress or frustration which is obviously very important if you're setting up something like a customer support bot or maybe like a sales type of agent If you want to watch the full breakdown then click on that play button right here
